title: centaurea pichleri subsp. extrarosularis
intro: Centaurea pichleri subsp. extrarosularis, also known as a rare subspecies of the Cornflower, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various gastrointestinal issues due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and potentially aiding in the treatment of certain infections. In herbal preparations, Centaurea pichleri subsp. extrarosularis is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used topically to treat wounds and internally to support digestive health.
